content:
Checking for that skill before I proceed.The `marketing-psychology` skill bundled here is also a catalogue stub — full workflow lives upstream. But I can apply the principles directly. Before I rewrite 8 files, let me propose the framework so you can redirect cheaply.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- Current campaign weakness: all 8 ads run the **same message** ("Your natural kick" + "-20% summer bundle"). That's a wasted A/B — you're testing visual treatment but not psychological lever, so any winner just tells you which layout, not which motivation drives clicks.
- A psych-driven redesign assigns a **distinct behavioral principle to each variant pair**, holding the GIMBER brand system + bottle fidelity + zone layouts constant. The visual chassis stays; the copy strategy and emphasis hierarchy change.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

## Proposed psychology framework (one lever per A/B pair)

| Placement | Variant A — principle | Variant B — principle |
|---|---|---|
| **Feed 1:1** | **Identity framing** — "For mornings that earn their light." (aspirational self-image, no promo) | **Loss aversion + scarcity** — "Summer bundle. -20%. Until stock runs." (deadline pressure) |
| **Feed 4:5** | **Specificity (cognitive ease)** — "3 ingredients. Zero crash. One kick." (concrete numbers beat adjectives) | **Anchoring** — show "€36 €28.80" with -20% as visible price strike (reference price makes saving feel real) |
| **Stories 9:16** | **Curiosity gap** — "Your morning doesn't need caffeine. Here's why." (open loop, swipe to resolve) | **Social proof + sensory** — "Belgium's natural kick. Cold-pressed ginger, lemon, agave." (authority + concrete sensory anchors) |
| **Carousel** | **Problem→agitation→solution** — F1 "3pm crash." F2 "Coffee again?" F3 "Or this." F4 "Your natural kick. -20%." | **Before/after transformation** — F1 morning grogginess → F2 the ritual → F3 the kick → F4 the day, won |

Cross-campaign psych moves layered on top:
- **Concrete > vague**: replace "natural energy" with ingredient names ("cold-pressed ginger, lemon, agave")
- **Verbs > nouns**: "Earn your morning" beats "Morning energy drink"
- **One CTA verb per ad** that matches the lever: *Claim* (scarcity), *Discover* (curiosity), *Switch* (identity), *Save* (anchoring)
- Promo patches stay yellow but only appear on variants where the lever is price-based (B-Feed1:1, B-Feed4:5, carousel f4) — A variants drop the promo entirely so the brand-led message can breathe

## Two questions before I build

<question-form id="psych-redesign" title="Psych redesign — confirm scope">
{
  "description": "Two quick calls before I rewrite all 8 files.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "scope",
      "label": "Apply the full psych framework above, or pick one lever to run across all 8?",
      "type": "radio",
      "required": true,
      "options": [
        "Full framework — different lever per A/B pair (recommended for real learning)",
        "One lever everywhere — pick the strongest and apply consistently",
        "Just rewrite copy, keep current visual hierarchy untouched"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "preserve",
      "label": "Anything to preserve as-is?",
      "type": "checkbox",
      "maxSelections": 4,
      "options": [
        "Keep stories-9x16-a (full-bleed bottle version) untouched",
        "Keep the -20% summer bundle promo on at least half the ads",
        "Keep 'Your Natural Kick' tagline asset on every ad",
        "Open to dropping the tagline asset where copy carries the lever"
      ]
    }
  ]
}
</question-form>
